element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Directional attack buttons (Left/Right) become core mechanic throughout game
Low-ranking melee enemies serve as foundational threat throughout
Introduces projectile threats and environmental weapon pickups
Tall, bare-chested heavy hitter; body vanishes on defeat but can rematerialize later
Only female enemy type; whip-exclusive fighter adds range threat
Tall enemy archetype continues from Mission 1

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game (specific mission unclear from sources)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: New special attack expanding player moveset

element_introduction_order.element: Bolo enemies (Oharra variant with longer hair)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Head-swap variant maintains enemy type escalation
Agile martial artist with kali sticks; weapon never drops when knocked down
Enemy matches all player moves; escalation of combat complexity
Black Warriors leader; uses machine gun never dropped; directly avenges Marian
Shadowy clone matching all player attacks plus energy drain ability; ultimate final battle
Story payload; three unused sprite variants suggest late development changes

typical_relief_triggers: Picking up thrown weapons (crates, logs, steel balls)
Reaching safe zones between enemy waves
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy variety increases (Linda's grenades, Jeff's full moveset)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating enemy complexity per mission with fixed timer pressure; boss encounters punctuate each stage; co-op mode enables defensive posturing via team positioning

difficulty_curve.early_game: Mission 1-2 introduce basic combat against Williams/Roper/Burnov/Abore; weapon pickup mechanics established via crate/log/ball throwing
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Missions 3-4 escalate with martial artist Chin Taimei, Jeff clones matching player moveset, and platform hazards (Mission 6 disappearing platforms); Willy introduces machine gun threat
difficulty_curve.late_game: Mission 9 final Doppelganger fight combines all learned mechanics with energy-drain mechanic; accessibility gated by difficulty setting in international versions (Supreme Master only)
notable_spikes: Chin Taimei boss (Mission 3) - kali stick weapon never drops
Jeff enemies (Mission 4) - mirrors full player moveset
Willy final boss (Mission 4) - machine gun never dropped
Doppelganger final battle - energy drain mechanic
Mission 6 disappearing platforms - timing puzzle under health pressure

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Mission structure with sub-bosses and final boss
notable_level_patterns.description: Each mission contains multiple Williams/Roper/Linda enemies, potentially other variants, and culminates in a named boss character (Burnov, Abore, Chin Taimei, Willy). Final mission adds story payload (Marian appearance) before Doppelganger.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mission 1: Heliport → Williams/Roper encounters → Burnov boss

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Enemy head-swaps and palette variants for escalation
notable_level_patterns.description: Oharra (Abobo head-swap) and Bolo (Oharra variant) repeat archetype with visual variation; Jeff is full Billy/Jimmy palette-swap with complete moveset. Maintains familiar silhouettes while signaling escalation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mission 2 (Abore) and Mission 3 (Chin Taimei) feature distinct new boss designs; Missions 4+ reuse Jeff as moveset escalation

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Environmental hazard introduction via platform mechanics
notable_level_patterns.description: Mission 6 features disappearing platforms with difficulty-dependent patterns (Easy: all visible; Normal: 2 at time; Difficult: 1 at time). Creates rhythm-based platforming puzzle layered over combat.
Early missions: knives, shovels, crates/logs. Mid-missions: whips, grenades, kali sticks. Late missions: machine guns, Doppelganger energy drain. Reflects enemy archetype escalation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Linda (whips/grenades) in Mission 2; Chin Taimei (kali sticks) Mission 3; Willy (machine gun) Mission 4

sources_used: tcrf - Double Dragon II: The Revenge (NES) - The Cutting Room Floor
